readme.purpose: Profile-specific deterministic market shocks layered on top of Monte Carlo returns. Use these to model known or assumed macro events at specific years.

mode_options.augment: Add these events on top of system_shocks.json preset events.
mode_options.replace: Ignore system_shocks.json entirely; only these events apply.

event_fields.depth: Maximum drawdown as a fraction. E.g. 0.30 = 30% peak-to-trough loss.
event_fields.dip_quarters: Number of quarters to reach maximum depth.
event_fields.recovery_quarters: Number of quarters to recover from trough.

override_mode_options.strict: This shock overrides any concurrent stochastic return for this class.
override_mode_options.augment: Shock is additive on top of the stochastic return.

recovery_to_options.baseline: Recover to the pre-shock trend line.
recovery_to_options.none: Recover to trough level only; no mean-reversion above trough.

type_options.poly: Polynomial curve. alpha > 1 = slow start then sharp. alpha < 1 = sharp start then slow.
type_options.exp: Exponential curve. lambda controls decay rate.

event_fields.coimpact_down: Other classes pulled down during the dip. mode: 'limited' (only listed classes), 'broad' (all equity classes), 'none'. scale: fraction of primary depth applied to co-impacted classes.
event_fields.corecovery_up: Other classes lifted during recovery. Same mode/scale options as coimpact_down. organic: true = co-recovering class follows its own profile shape. organic_profile: dip/rise profile for the organic recovery.
event_fields.correlated_to: For co-movement shocks: this class moves as a scaled version of another class's shock. Use with scale field.
event_fields.scale: Fraction of the correlated class's movement applied to this class.
